predictions.text: NASA will launch Artemis II (crewed mission) by 2026-04-05 (inclusive).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if NASA (or NASA TV/webcast archive) confirms Artemis II liftoff by 2026-04-05 23:59 ET; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: NASA Artemis II official schedule updates
Launch readiness review (LRR) outcomes
Weather/technical scrub announcements at Kennedy Space Center
predictions.rationale: NASA states April 1 is on track, but crewed launches often slip due to technical and range constraints.

predictions.text: Adobe will file an 8-K (or publish an equivalent investor release) formally naming its next CEO by 2026-06-30.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if Adobe files an SEC 8-K/10-Q/10-K or issues an investor press release naming a successor CEO (not merely interim) by the deadline; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Adobe SEC filings (8-K) for leadership changes
Adobe investor relations press releases
Board/executive announcements in earnings materials
predictions.rationale: Publicly announced step-downs typically coincide with an identified successor within 1–2 quarters.

predictions.text: Meta will publicly launch a new flagship Llama major-version model (marketed as a new generation beyond its current flagship) by 2026-06-30.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if Meta releases a new major-version Llama model (e.g., 'Llama 4' or equivalent explicitly positioned as the next flagship generation) via official Meta/Meta AI channels by the deadline; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Meta AI blog announcements
GitHub/model card releases under Meta branding
Conference keynotes and Meta earnings-call statements on model release timing
predictions.rationale: Delay signals execution risk, but competitive pressure makes a flagship release within months plausible.

predictions.text: The FOMC will not cut the federal funds target range at any meeting through (and including) the 2026-06-17 FOMC decision.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if every FOMC decision from 2026-03-18 through 2026-06-17 leaves the target range unchanged or higher versus the prior meeting; NO if any cut occurs by 2026-06-17.
monitoring_signals: CME FedWatch probabilities for 2026 meetings
Monthly CPI/PCE prints and inflation expectations surveys
Energy price pass-through indicators (gasoline, airline fares)
predictions.rationale: Oil-supply shock and inflation risk reduce near-term easing odds unless growth deteriorates rapidly.

predictions.text: The U.S. Navy (or DoD) will publicly confirm at least one escorted transit of a commercial oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz by 2026-04-30.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if a DoD/Navy press release, briefing transcript, or official social media statement confirms an escort operation for a named (or clearly identified) commercial oil tanker through Hormuz by the deadline; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: CENTCOM/5th Fleet press briefings
AIS tracking of convoys and reported naval escorts
Statements from major tanker owners/charterers about escorted voyages
predictions.rationale: Announced intent plus high disruption risk makes at least one confirmed escort likely within weeks.

predictions.text: OFAC (or U.S. Treasury) will extend or expand written authorization allowing purchases/transactions involving Russian-origin crude oil already loaded ('at sea') for market-stabilization purposes by 2026-05-15.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if OFAC issues a new/updated General License, FAQ, or formal guidance after 2026-03-13 that explicitly extends eligibility (time window, counterparties, or scope) for Russian crude already loaded/at sea; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: OFAC General Licenses and FAQ updates
Treasury/State press briefings on sanctions enforcement flexibility
Reporting on stranded cargo volumes and price dislocations
predictions.rationale: Emergency flexibility is plausible but politically sensitive; extension depends on market stress severity.

predictions.text: USTR will publish at least one Federal Register notice proposing Section 301 trade remedies (additional duties and/or import restrictions) explicitly tied to the March 2026 forced-labor probes, covering at least 3 named economies and at least $10B in total referenced annual import value by 2026-06-30.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if a Federal Register notice (or USTR notice) proposes remedies under Section 301 that explicitly reference the forced-labor probes and lists ≥3 economies with combined cited annual import value ≥$10B; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Federal Register searches for USTR Section 301 notices
USTR press releases on investigation timelines
Industry comment periods and hearing schedules
predictions.rationale: Launching broad probes raises odds of remedies, but procedural steps can push actions past June.

predictions.text: Fitch Ratings will publish a report/press release stating that the U.S. private credit default rate (as defined by Fitch) reached or exceeded 10.0% for any trailing-12-month period ending by 2026-06-30.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if Fitch explicitly reports a U.S. private credit default rate ≥10.0% for a trailing-12-month period with an end date on or before 2026-06-30; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Fitch private credit default updates
BDC/credit fund earnings with non-accrual increases
Refinancing volumes and distressed-exchange activity
predictions.rationale: Defaults were already near record levels in 2025; further tightening and slowdown could push into double digits.

predictions.text: FDA will grant Breakthrough Device Designation to at least one blood-based test intended to detect or aid early detection of Alzheimer’s disease by 2026-12-31.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if an FDA database update or company press release confirms Breakthrough Device Designation for a blood-based Alzheimer’s detection test by the deadline; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Company PRs from major diagnostics firms
FDA Breakthrough Devices program announcements
Large prospective validation study readouts for blood biomarkers
predictions.rationale: Scientific momentum is strong, but regulatory designation timing depends on sponsor submissions and evidence packages.

predictions.text: A Phase 1 or Phase 2 interventional clinical trial of a narrow-spectrum antibiotic specifically for Clostridioides difficile (C. diff) infection that claims microbiome-sparing properties will be newly registered on ClinicalTrials.gov by 2026-12-31.
predictions.verification_criteria: YES if ClinicalTrials.gov posts a new Phase 1/2 interventional study for a C. diff antibiotic with sponsor materials describing microbiome-sparing/narrow-spectrum intent by the deadline; otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: ClinicalTrials.gov new registrations mentioning C. difficile and microbiome-sparing
Biotech pipeline announcements in anti-infectives
NIH/BARDA funding awards for C. diff therapeutics
predictions.rationale: Promising preclinical/early results often translate into early-phase trials within a year, but financing and tox packages can delay.
